title: Sony's decision to eliminate physical discs on PlayStation sparks controversy and reactions
summary: The end of the physical format prompts calls for legislation, industry criticism, and alternative proposals such as code preservation on CD-ROM.

title: The Steam Machine suffers critical GPU failures known as 'red line of death'
summary: Several units present a hardware error that renders them unusable within minutes of use, similar to the Xbox 360's red ring.

title: Microsoft secretly develops an operating system based entirely on Copilot and AI agents
summary: The project called 'Windows Project Aion' seeks to integrate the AI assistant into the core of the system.

title: A member of the European Parliament investigating espionage abuses was hacked using Pegasus spyware
summary: The attack against a member of the European Parliament underscores the use of surveillance software against political targets.

title: Artificial intelligence boosts vulnerability detection and enables new types of cyberattacks
summary: The rise of AI models has increased security flaws, while AI-powered browsers can be manipulated to execute attacks.

title: Intel fixes performance issues in its 18A node and accelerates wafer production
summary: The company claims to have resolved manufacturing defects in its 18A process, reaching up to 30,000 wafers per month.

title: Intel prepares Nova Lake-S processors with 22 cores and special cache for gaming
summary: The new Core Ultra 400 will include 125W and 65W versions with a cache that promises to improve performance in video games.

title: Intel confirms price increases on its Core and Xeon processors due to rising costs
summary: The company applies increases of up to $1,000 on some Xeon models and raises prices on Core Ultra 7 and Ultra 5.

title: Chevrolet's electric pickup truck fails to convince the US market
summary: Despite being a 100% US-manufactured vehicle, sales are not taking off and the company seeks to understand why.

title: Tesla limits its employees' weekly spending on AI tools to $200
summary: The measure aims to control the use of artificial intelligence within the company, according to internal reports.

title: Mark Zuckerberg acknowledges that the development of AI agents at Meta is progressing slower than expected
summary: The CEO of Meta admits that initial expectations about autonomous agents have not been met as planned.

title: Blackstone cancels the world's largest data center campus project due to a legal technicality
summary: The 2,100-acre Digital Gateway project in Virginia is discarded after years of lawsuits and a defect in public notification.

title: A Tesla driver is charged with homicide after an accident with FSD mode activated
summary: The accident occurred when the vehicle in full self-driving mode crashed into a home in Texas.

title: The EU imposes a €4.1 billion fine on Google for abuse of dominant position with Android
summary: The Court of Justice of the European Union confirms the penalty for monopolistic practices in the mobile operating system market.

title: Scientists manage to reverse the flow of time in a quantum experiment
summary: A team of physicists manages to make time flow backwards at the quantum level, opening new possibilities in computing.

title: A fungus inside Chernobyl feeds on radiation and could have space applications
summary: The radiotrophic organism detected in the nuclear reactor could be used to protect astronauts from radiation.

title: Negotiations for the unionization of Google DeepMind begin with difficulties
summary: Employees of the AI lab seek to form a union, but the company shows resistance to conversations.

title: UK AI safety institute warns that benchmarks underestimate agents' capabilities
summary: Standard tests do not reflect the true potential of autonomous artificial intelligence agents.

title: Chinese company Kling raises $2 billion for AI video expansion
summary: The AI video generation company prepares for its IPO in Hong Kong.

title: Anthropic develops its own AI chip to compete with NVIDIA and other companies
summary: The company behind Claude seeks to strengthen its AI leadership with a processor specifically designed for its models.
